summary: Ukraine reported a successful strike on a Russian UAV training centre in occupied Snizhne, killing 65 personnel. Russian sources listed extensive drone activity across multiple regions, including downed UAVs in Belgorod, Stavropol and Leningrad oblasts. Zelensky warned of five Russian scenarios for a northern offensive from Belarus toward Kyiv.
key_points: Ukrainian drones destroyed the Russian UAV training centre in Snizhne, Donetsk, killing 65 operators and the chief "Buryi" (UkraineNow/81356).
Russian Defence Ministry reported 651 enemy drones downed, with attacks on Nevinnomyssk (Stavropol), Belgorod missile strike, and oil refinery in Kstovo (Russian report/30306).
Shahed loitering munitions were observed targeting Ivano‑Frankivsk (Kolomyia), Chernihiv (Holmy), and Kropyvnytskyi regions (mon1tor_ua/66852, 66853).
Multiple small UAV groups ("BPLA") were tracked moving from southern Khmelnytskyi toward Chernivtsi and from northern Zaporizhzhia toward Zaporizhzhia city (kpszsu/62318).
Polish defence ministry demanded tighter Ukrainian drone targeting after a UAV entered Estonian airspace (ukr_leaks_eng/30314).
Zelensky warned Russia has five scenarios for expanding the war via a Belarus‑based offensive toward Chernigov‑Kyiv corridor (ukr_leaks_eng/30315).
Russian forces reported advancing up to 600 m on the Sumy front and ongoing offensives in East‑Zaporizhia and Kupyansk directions (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
NATO Secretary‑General warned of a "devastating" response to any Russian nuclear use, noting ongoing nuclear drills in Belarus (UkraineNow/81351).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones eliminated a Russian UAV training centre in Snizhne, killing 65, marking a significant degradation of Russian UAV capabilities. Russia reported extensive drone engagements, downing 651 UAVs across several oblasts while continuing ground offensives in Sumy, East‑Zaporizhia and Kupyansk. Zelensky warned of five possible Russian northern thrusts from Belarus toward Kyiv, underscoring heightened threat on the northern front.

points: Ukrainian strike on the Russian UAV training centre in Snizhne (occupied Donetsk) eliminated 65 trainees and the centre chief, using 11 strike drones each carrying 100 kg payloads (UkraineNow/81356).
Ukrainian forces reported Shahed attacks on Ivano‑Frankivsk (Kolomyia) and Kropyvnytskyi, with at least three loitering munitions observed (mon1tor_ua/66850, 66852).

Ukrainian drone operators tracked multiple BPLA groups moving from southern Khmelnytskyi toward Chernivtsi and from northern Zaporizhzhia toward Zaporizhzhia city, indicating coordinated reconnaissance (kpszsu/62318).
Polish defence ministry urged Kyiv to refine target selection after a UAV entered Estonian airspace, highlighting NATO airspace security concerns (ukr_leaks_eng/30314).
Ukrainian forces claimed advances of up to 600 m on the Sumy front, with 19 sectors seeing assault unit movement (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian reports indicated continued UAV attacks on civilian infrastructure in Kherson, causing power outages in nine districts (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian Defence Ministry announced 651 enemy drones downed, citing interceptions over Nevinnomyssk (Stavropol), Belgorod missile strike, and oil refinery attacks in Kstovo (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian reports detailed UAV shoot‑downs in Leningrad, Tula, Rostov and Bryansk regions, with civilian casualties from drone‑detonated trucks and cars (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian ground forces continued offensive actions on the East‑Zaporizhia front, aiming to capture Verkhnya Tersa, Vozdvizhevka and Komsomolskoe (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
In the Kupyansk sector, Russian troops are breaking Ukrainian defenses along the Oskol River east bank, indicating sustained pressure in the south (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian forces reported the loss of Krasnoarmeysk (Pokrovsk) but claimed ongoing operations in Vasilevka and Mirnye (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian air defence claimed successful repulsion of a drone attack on an industrial zone in Nevinnomyssk, demonstrating continued air‑defence activity (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian assault units of the 126th Motor Rifle Regiment recaptured Volokhovka and liberated the settlement on the Kharkiv front (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian forces reported advancing up to 600 m on the Sumy direction across nineteen sectors, indicating localized gains (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAV attacks caused civilian injuries in Veliki Kopany, Kakhovka and near Rykovo within the Kherson region (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian reports of drone attacks on oil refinery in Kstovo (Nizhny Novgorod region) show extended strike reach beyond the immediate front (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian forces claimed destruction of a Russian logistics hub in the Dobropillia direction, disrupting supply lines (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Zelensky warned of five Russian scenarios for expanding the war via a Belarus‑based offensive toward the Chernigov‑Kyiv axis (ukr_leaks_eng/30315).
Russian drone activity reported over Belgorod and Kursk regions, with multiple UAV interceptions indicating heightened aerial contest (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian intelligence noted Shahed loitering munitions heading toward Chernihiv (Holmy) and Sumy‑Kharkiv border areas (kpszsu/62318, 62337).
Russian forces continued artillery and UAV strikes on Ukrainian positions in the north, including downed drones over Leningrad and Tula regions (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Polish defence ministry’s demand for precise Ukrainian targeting reflects NATO’s concern over northern airspace incursions (ukr_leaks_eng/30314).
Russian offensive actions persisted on the Kupyansk front along the Oskol River east bank, aiming to breach Ukrainian defenses (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukrainian UAV strikes caused power outages in nine districts of Kherson, indicating effective southern air‑strike capability (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Shahed munitions were observed heading toward Kropyvnytskyi (Bashan district) from the south, suggesting pressure on central‑southern regions (mon1tor_ua/66852).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying Russian logistics nodes in the Dobropillia area, affecting southern supply routes (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian reports of UAV attacks on civilian trucks in Belgorod and Shebekino highlight cross‑border strike risks to southern Ukrainian territories (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Ukraine employed 11 strike drones with 100 kg warheads to hit the Snizhne UAV training centre, achieving 65 casualties (UkraineNow/81356).
Russia claimed to have downed 651 Ukrainian drones across multiple oblasts, reflecting intense aerial contest (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Multiple Shahed loitering munitions were tracked heading toward Ivano‑Frankivsk, Kropyvnytskyi and Chernihiv, indicating continued use of Iranian‑supplied systems (mon1tor_ua/66852, 66853).

Ukrainian UAV groups (BPLA) were observed moving from southern Khmelnytskyi toward Chernivtsi and from northern Zaporizhzhia toward Zaporizhzhia city, suggesting reconnaissance and strike missions (kpszsu/62318).
Russian air‑defence repelled a drone attack on an industrial zone in Nevinnomyssk, showing effective point‑defence capabilities (ukr_leaks_eng/30306).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones eliminated the Russian UAV training centre in Snizhne, Donetsk, killing 65 operators and the centre chief (Source: @UkraineNow/81356).
Russian Defence Ministry reported downing 651 Ukrainian drones across multiple regions, including Nevinnomyssk, Belgorod and Kstovo (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Zelensky warned of five Russian scenarios for a northern offensive from Belarus toward the Chernigov‑Kyiv corridor (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30315).
Shahed loitering munitions were observed targeting Ivano‑Frankivsk (Kolomyia) and Kropyvnytskyi, indicating continued Iranian‑supplied strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).
Polish defence ministry demanded tighter Ukrainian drone targeting after a UAV entered Estonian airspace, highlighting NATO airspace concerns (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30314).
Ukrainian forces reported advances of up to 600 m on the Sumy front across nineteen sectors, showing localized gains (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
Russian offensive operations continued on the East‑Zaporizhia front aiming to capture Verkhnya Tersa, Vozdvizhevka and Komsomolskoe (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30306).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian plans for a Belarus‑originated northern thrust increase strategic risk to Kyiv and surrounding oblasts, while sustained UAV engagements on both sides strain air‑defence resources. Ukraine's successful strike on the Snizhne UAV training centre degrades Russian drone capabilities but does not halt their extensive use. Continued Shahed attacks and Russian ground offensives in the south and east maintain high operational pressure across multiple fronts.
sections.regional_response: NATO allies, notably Poland, are urging stricter Ukrainian targeting to prevent incidents in NATO airspace, reflecting heightened alliance vigilance. NATO's explicit warning of a "devastating" response to any Russian nuclear use underscores the broader geopolitical stakes. Regional partners are monitoring increased drone activity, with potential adjustments to air‑defence postures along eastern and northern borders.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source UAV tracking indicates coordinated BPLA movements from southern Khmelnytskyi toward Chernivtsi and from northern Zaporizhzhia toward Zaporizhzhia, suggesting reconnaissance for upcoming offensives. Russian reports of extensive drone losses may be inflated for propaganda, yet the volume of engagements points to a saturated aerial battlespace. Monitoring of Belarusian troop concentrations near the Chernigov‑Kyiv axis is critical given Zelensky's warning of multiple offensive scenarios.
